title: Add a SIP Provider
lede: Connect your own SIP trunk or carrier so iMash can send and receive phone calls through it. Most users can skip this — iMash numbers already come with calling built in. Use this only if you bring your own carrier.

steps.title: Open SIP Providers
steps.content:
1. In the left menu, go to Call Center → SIP Providers.
  2. Click Add Provider (or the + button) to open the Create New SIP Provider panel.

A SIP provider is your outside phone carrier (for example your own Telnyx, Twilio, or on-prem PBX trunk). Connecting one lets iMash route calls over it instead of buying numbers through iMash.

images.caption: Call Center → SIP Providers is where you connect an outside carrier.
Call Center → SIP Providers is where you connect an outside carrier.

steps.title: Name it and whitelist iMash
steps.content:
1. Enter a Provider Name (for example "My Telnyx Trunk") so you can recognize it later.
  2. On your carrier's side, whitelist the iMash SIP signaling IP shown in the panel — 34.150.248.33:5060 — so your carrier accepts calls to and from iMash.

images.caption: Give the trunk a name and whitelist the iMash signaling IP on your carrier.
Give the trunk a name and whitelist the iMash signaling IP on your carrier.

steps.title: Add your SIP gateways
steps.content:
A gateway tells iMash where your carrier lives on the network.

  1. Under SIP gateways, fill in the Network address (an IP such as 1.2.3.4, or for outbound a domain like sip.mycarrier.com), the Port (usually 5060), and the Netmask.
  2. Pick a direction: Inbound (calls coming in) or Outbound (calls going out). You can add several inbound gateways but only one outbound gateway.
  3. Click Add New Gateway to add more as needed.

steps.title: Add authentication (optional)
steps.content:
If your carrier requires a username and password:

  1. Open the Authentication section.
  2. Enter the Auth username and Password.
  3. Tick Require Auth for Incoming and/or Require Auth for Outgoing if your carrier expects credentials in that direction.

steps.title: Create the provider
steps.content:
1. Review your entries — the Create Provider button stays disabled until the required fields are valid.
  2. Click Create Provider.
  3. Your trunk now appears in the SIP Providers list. When you import a number under this provider, iMash provisions the routing automatically.

nextSteps.title: What's Next?
nextSteps.description: With a trunk connected, add a number and point calls at an agent.

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference

metaTitle: Add a SIP Provider - Connect Your Carrier | iMash Docs
metaDescription: Connect your own SIP trunk or carrier to iMash. Set the provider name, whitelist the signaling IP, add inbound/outbound gateways, and authentication.
